title: Percent, Rates, Markups, Discounts, and Repeated Change · Worked Case: Markup Discount
summary: Audit prices, discounts, taxes, tips, population changes, and performance claims. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg markup discount increment in a consumer percent-claim audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model markup, discount, tax, and tip as ordered multiplicative factors.
Calculate absolute and relative change and explain when each comparison is useful.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Markup Discount evidence
steps.instruction: A price of $90 is reduced by 12%. What is the new price? Use the result to decide Markup Discount for a consumer percent-claim audit.
steps.why: The Markup Discount result for a consumer percent-claim audit is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use 90 × (1 − 12/100), then compare the result with the original price. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model markup, discount, tax, and tip as ordered multiplicative factors.

content.text: Before calculating Markup Discount, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a consumer percent-claim audit. hints: Use 90 × (1 − 12/100), then compare the result with the original price.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.
